content.metaDescription: Learn why EncouragingYou exists, how its youth-led mission took shape, and how that purpose connects to practical support across Rochdale.

intro.title: A youth-led organisation rooted in real community experience.
intro.summary: We created EncouragingYou to be the kind of support we wished existed: welcoming, practical, inclusive, and real.

storySections.title: EncouragingYou started with a gap we could already see around us.
paragraphs: The organisation began after seeing young people in the local community struggling, feeling lost, or not knowing where to turn for steady support.

Instead of building something distant or formal, the aim was to create the kind of space we wished had existed already: somewhere people could feel understood, welcomed, and given a real chance to grow.

quote.text: We wanted to create the kind of support we wished existed: welcoming, practical, inclusive, and real.

storySections.title: Run by young leaders who understand the pressures first-hand.
paragraphs: EncouragingYou is run by dedicated young leaders and volunteers. That matters because the people shaping the work are close to the realities many young people are facing now, not talking about them from a distance.
That lived experience helps the organisation connect in a more relatable, supportive, and authentic way. The goal is not to position leadership above the community, but to grow alongside it.
bullets: Youth-led leadership shapes the tone, priorities, and atmosphere.
Practical help sits alongside belonging and confidence-building.
People can understand the next step without gatekeeping.

storySections.title: Support that is clear, low-pressure, and useful from the first visit.
paragraphs: The organisation brings together practical support such as CV help, confidence-building, and mentoring with a community atmosphere that helps people feel they belong.
That means short routes into help, plain language, and visible next steps rather than asking people to prove themselves before they are welcomed.
bullets: Practical help and confidence-building sit together.
Belonging matters as much as information.
Privacy and safeguarding routes stay visible near contact points.

note.title: Trust in practice
note.body: This route builds confidence through clear public information. Named team biographies, partner proof, and impact claims stay out of the page until they are approved for publication.

storySections.title: The work is youth-led, but the welcome is wider than youth-only support.
paragraphs: The strongest focus remains around young people, confidence, and practical support, but the vision is for a stronger and more connected community overall.
That opens space for community events, social connection, and support that can also reach older generations or people who might otherwise feel isolated.

note.title: Current public scope
note.body: The community direction is public now. Fuller examples, partner references, and future event detail can be added later once approved for publication.

purposeSection.title: Purpose first, with community at the centre.
purposeSection.summary: The mission stays practical: help people feel supported, connected, and able to move forward.
purposeSection.driveSummary: The work is driven by seeing young people gain confidence, succeed, and feel valued, while the wider community becomes more connected and more able to support one another.
driveBullets: Create a welcoming environment where people feel comfortable rather than judged.
Help people connect, belong, build confidence, and support each other.
Keep the community focus broad enough to include younger and older generations.

valuesSection.title: What people should feel in the way we work.
valuesSection.summary: These are practical standards for how support is delivered, not abstract slogans.

items.title: Welcome without judgement
items.summary: The atmosphere feels calm, respectful, and easy to enter, especially for people who are unsure about asking for help.

items.title: Real support, not empty promises
items.summary: Advice, mentoring, and next steps are practical enough to help someone move forward, not just sound encouraging.

items.title: Community connection
items.summary: Belonging matters. Support helps people feel part of something rather than left to figure everything out alone.

items.title: Confidence and opportunity
items.summary: The work helps people grow in confidence, recognise their strengths, and access new opportunities.

items.title: Respect, safety, and inclusion
items.summary: Clear boundaries, privacy guidance, and safeguarding routes are part of the welcome, not an afterthought.

audienceSection.title: Support that reaches beyond one age group or one kind of need.
audienceSection.summary: The strongest fit is still around youth support, confidence, and guidance, but the wider community matters too.

items.title: Young people looking for support or direction
items.summary: This includes people who want confidence-building, practical help, mentoring, or a place where they can feel supported and understood.

items.title: Parents, carers, referrers, and community members
items.summary: The route should also work for people trying to help someone else take the next step, ask a question, or understand what support is available.

items.title: Older or isolated community members who need connection
items.summary: The wider community commitment leaves room for intergenerational support, shared activities, and routes that reduce isolation rather than treating people as separate audiences.

trustSection.title: Confidence should come from clear routes and visible care, not inflated claims.
trustSection.summary: The strongest public trust signals stay close to the story: youth-led leadership, a welcoming environment, privacy clarity, and a visible safeguarding path.

proofBoundary.title: What we are not claiming in public yet.
proofBoundary.summary: The page stays warm and complete without pretending certainty where proof still needs approval.
publishNow: The origin story, mission, vision, and youth-led posture already described on the public site.
Practical support pathways, privacy guidance, and safeguarding signposts already visible across the site.
awaitingConfirmation: Any public wording that depends on confirmed vetting or training process detail.
Named leadership biographies, team portraits, and approved partner references.
withheldUntilVerified: Impact statistics, testimonials, or outcome claims that have not been approved.
Detailed claims about live partnerships, referral volume, or specific venue disclosure.

pathwaysSection.title: Different people help in different ways.

items.title: Help create a calm, welcoming environment.

Helping around employability, confidence, or creative activity when appropriate
Starting with a conversation about fit before any role is agreed

items.title: Offer lighter-touch or one-off help when regular volunteering is not realistic.
items.summary: Not every contribution needs to begin as a long-term commitment. Sometimes the best first step is occasional help, local introductions, or practical support around community activity.
bullets: One-off or occasional support can still be useful

supportSection.title: Support should come before commitment.

items.body: The first conversation covers why you want to help, what kind of role feels realistic, and where boundaries or extra support might be needed.

items.body: If a role looks like a fit, day-to-day expectations, safeguarding context, and who you would be working alongside are explained before anything starts.

items.title: Questions are welcome before agreement
items.body: The route stays open to people who want to ask first, especially if they are new to volunteering or unsure what kind of role would suit them.

processSection.title: A fair first conversation comes before any role is agreed.
processSection.summary: The enquiry flow is designed to stay low-pressure while still protecting the organisation, the people involved, and the safeguarding boundary.

steps.title: Send a short enquiry
steps.body: Tell us how you would like to help, what feels realistic, and whether you are asking about a regular or occasional contribution.

steps.title: We reply with the right first conversation
steps.body: You get a response that routes the next conversation properly rather than forcing every volunteer into the same generic path.

steps.title: Checks and support are explained before anything starts
steps.body: If a role involves safeguarding responsibilities or a higher level of trust, that is discussed clearly alongside support, supervision, and next steps.

screeningSection.title: Safeguarding is part of the route, not fine print.
screeningSection.summary: The public material supports clear safeguarding visibility, but it does not justify pretending every role has the same process or level of vetting.

items.title: Keep the safety boundary visible early
items.body: If a pathway would place someone around young people or other sensitive community support, safeguarding expectations are visible before anyone is asked to proceed.

items.title: Checks depend on the level of responsibility
items.body: Not every volunteer pathway needs the same checks. If a role requires more screening, that is explained proportionately and clearly before commitment.

items.title: Use the verified public trust statement
items.body: The public safeguarding statement confirms that EncouragingYou follows safeguarding procedures and uses trained, vetted staff and volunteers.

note.title: Need the full safeguarding route?
note.body: Use the dedicated safeguarding page if your question is about a concern, the safety process itself, or the difference between child and adult safeguarding routes.

timeCommitmentSection.title: Start with what you can genuinely offer.
timeCommitmentSection.summary: A realistic offer of time is more useful than a bigger promise that becomes hard to sustain.

items.title: Honesty is better than over-committing
items.body: If you can only help occasionally or in a lighter-touch way, say that early. The first conversation matches the route to what is genuinely possible.

items.title: Regular and occasional roles are not the same
items.body: Some support may need steadier availability while other contributions may be more flexible. The route explains that difference instead of hiding it.

items.title: A slower start can still be the right start
items.body: First-time volunteers, younger helpers, or people returning after a gap may need a clearer introduction and a gentler pace at the beginning.

faqSection.title: Questions people often ask before volunteering.

items.answer: No. Public information supports community connection, wider support, community events, and practical guidance. It does not support claims about regulated care, home visits, or specialist services beyond that public scope.

items.question: Can I ask about volunteering before applying?
items.answer: Yes. The route stays practical and transparent, with clear expectations before anyone is asked for a long-form application.

items.question: Do partnership enquiries have to be formal?
items.answer: No. The site supports low-friction first contact for referrals, collaboration ideas, and local partnership conversations.

items.question: Can I ask on behalf of someone else?
items.answer: Yes. Parents, carers, schools, and community partners can use the referral-friendly routes and explain that they are asking on someone else's behalf.

items.question: What if I can help but I do not fit one named route yet?
items.answer: That is okay. Use the contact-led supporter path and explain what kind of practical help, introduction, space, or local support you have in mind.

items.question: Can I ask questions before I commit to anything?
items.answer: Yes. The first step is a short enquiry and a fair conversation about fit, support, and next steps. There is no pressure to promise more than you can offer.

items.question: Do I need formal experience to ask about volunteering?
items.answer: Not always. The public route supports practical help, encouragement, and community-minded involvement. The right fit depends on the role, the current need, and the support required.

items.question: Will every volunteer role involve the same checks?
items.answer: No. Safeguarding conversations and checks depend on the level of responsibility involved. If anything is needed for a role, it is explained clearly before anything starts.

items.question: What if I can only help occasionally?
items.answer: Say that in your first message. One-off or lighter-touch help can still be useful, and it is better to be honest about your availability than to over-commit.

items.question: Do partnership enquiries need to be formal from the start?
items.answer: No. The first step can stay simple. A short outline of the organisation, local need, collaboration idea, or practical offer is enough to start the right conversation.

items.question: Can a school or organisation ask on behalf of a young person or family?
items.answer: Yes. If the conversation is really about someone needing support, say that clearly so the team can route it as a referral or support enquiry rather than a general partnership discussion.

items.question: Do you already publish partner logos, packages, or case studies?
items.answer: Not yet. The route stays clear without publishing unapproved logos, case studies, or testimonials.

items.question: Is this route only for funding or sponsorship conversations?
items.answer: No. It is also for referrals, joint delivery ideas, venue support, in-kind help, community collaboration, and other practical local partnerships.

page.title: How this site handles the personal information people share with us.
page.summary: This notice covers the live EncouragingYou website routes where someone can send a message, ask for support, enquire about volunteering or partnership, or raise a non-emergency safeguarding concern.

statusNote.title: Current build scope
statusNote.body: This notice is tied to the launch build that is live in the repo now. It covers the secure enquiry routes, the same-site form storage, and the live first-party aggregate analytics layer described in the Cookie Notice.

summaryCards.title: What the site collects now
summaryCards.body: The live forms ask only for the details needed to read a message and reply properly.
bullets: Name
Email address
Reason for contact
Message content
Optional updates choice where that box is shown

summaryCards.title: What the site does not do at launch
summaryCards.body: The current build does not wire these routes into extra tracking or marketing systems.
bullets: No advertising or cross-site analytics cookies on the live routes
No third-party analytics platform or tag manager on the live routes
No CRM or case-management platform declared in the repo
No separate newsletter platform connected to the updates opt-in
No interactive map on the contact page by default

summaryCards.title: Where privacy is shown
summaryCards.body: The site repeats a short privacy note where people actually enter details, not only in the footer.
bullets: Home and Contact forms
Get Involved, Volunteer, and Partner forms
Accessibility feedback form
Safeguarding secure concern form
Footer and legal navigation for the full notice

contents.label: Who we are and how to contact us
What information the site collects now
The live forms and enquiry routes
Where messages go and what systems are connected

collectionPointSection.title: The live forms and enquiry routes on the current build.
collectionPointSection.summary: Each route uses the same short-form contact pattern unless the form makes the difference explicit. Safeguarding is handled separately because it may involve more sensitive information.

collectionPoints.title: General contact and session follow-up
collectionPoints.purpose: To reply, route the next step, and keep any session context attached when someone arrives from a session journey.
collectionPoints.workingBasis: Someone has asked us to reply or help with a next step.
collectionPoints.sharing: Handled by the EncouragingYou team through the site's enquiry storage. No separate CRM or marketing platform is configured for this route at launch.
collectionPoints.retention: Reviewed manually and deleted when the conversation is complete unless we need to keep a limited record for an active service, safeguarding issue, or legal reason.
collectionPoints.note: The optional updates box is separate from the main support message.

collectionPoints.title: Get Involved hub messages
collectionPoints.purpose: To route a message into the right volunteer, partner, referral, or general involvement conversation.
collectionPoints.workingBasis: Someone has asked us to route them into the right involvement pathway.
collectionPoints.sharing: Handled internally by the team responsible for the first response. The updates choice is stored with the enquiry rather than sent to a separate newsletter tool.
collectionPoints.retention: Reviewed manually and deleted when the involvement conversation is complete unless a clear operational reason remains.
collectionPoints.note: This route should not be used for urgent safeguarding concerns.

collectionPoints.title: Volunteer enquiries
collectionPoints.purpose: To understand how someone would like to help and decide the fairest next volunteer conversation to offer.
collectionPoints.workingBasis: Someone has asked us to respond to a volunteering enquiry.
collectionPoints.sharing: Handled by the team reviewing volunteer interest. No volunteer CRM, rota platform, or external recruitment tool is declared in the repo at launch.
collectionPoints.retention: Reviewed manually and deleted when the volunteer conversation is closed unless we need to retain a limited record for safeguarding, screening, or legal reasons.

collectionPoints.title: Partner, referral, and collaboration enquiries
collectionPoints.purpose: To understand a collaboration, referral, sponsorship, or community offer and route it to the right follow-up.
collectionPoints.workingBasis: Someone has asked us to continue a partnership or referral conversation.
collectionPoints.sharing: Handled internally by the team reviewing partnership and referral messages. No separate partner-management tool is declared in the launch repo.
collectionPoints.retention: Reviewed manually and deleted when the conversation is complete unless a live partnership, referral, safeguarding matter, or legal duty means we need to keep a limited record.

collectionPoints.title: Safeguarding concern form
collectionPoints.purpose: To review and route a non-emergency safeguarding concern and contact the sender back if that is needed.
collectionPoints.workingBasis: We may need to protect someone, assess a concern responsibly, and keep a record of the action taken.
collectionPoints.sharing: Shared only as needed with the people responsible for reviewing or escalating the concern. If safety requires it, information may be passed to the appropriate safeguarding, referral, or emergency service.
collectionPoints.retention: Kept for as long as needed to manage the concern, any follow-up, and any safeguarding or legal record-keeping duty. We do not publish a fixed deletion window for safeguarding records.
collectionPoints.note: This form does not include the optional updates box.

collectionPoints.title: Accessibility feedback form
collectionPoints.purpose: To understand an accessibility barrier, reply, and offer a practical fix or alternative format where possible.
collectionPoints.workingBasis: Someone has asked us for accessibility help or reported a barrier on the site.
collectionPoints.sharing: Handled internally by the team reviewing the website and public enquiries. No separate ticketing, CRM, or accessibility platform is declared in the launch repo.
collectionPoints.retention: Reviewed manually and deleted when the accessibility issue is resolved unless a longer record is needed to evidence a live issue, safeguarding concern, or legal reason.
collectionPoints.note: This form does not include the optional updates box.

sections.title: Who we are and how to contact us
paragraphs: EncouragingYou uses this notice for the public website routes where people can contact the team, ask about support, enquire about volunteering or partnership, or raise a non-emergency safeguarding concern.
The quickest privacy contact published on the site today is admin@encouragingyou.co.uk. People can also use the Contact route if they would rather start with the form.
If your message is really about someone's immediate safety, do not rely on an ordinary contact route or wait for a reply. Use the safeguarding route or call 999 in an emergency.

note.title: Privacy contact
note.body: Use the Contact route for ordinary privacy questions, or email the published address if that is easier.

sections.title: What information the site collects now
sections.summary: The public forms stay deliberately short. The site also records limited technical details to protect the form against abuse.
paragraphs: When someone uses a live form, the site stores the details they typed into the form together with the route they used and, where relevant, the session context that sent them into Contact.

To protect the form from spam and misuse, the site also checks the route where the form was loaded, the form render time, and a hidden honeypot field. The stored enquiry record keeps a hashed version of the requester IP where available, along with user-agent, origin, and referer information.
The site does not ask for postal address, date of birth, payment information, or account credentials on these public routes.
bullets: Direct form fields: name, email, reason, message, and optional updates preference where shown
Technical anti-abuse data: origin path, render time, hashed IP where available, user-agent, origin, and referer
Session context handoff for the Contact route when someone comes from CV Support or Youth Club

sections.title: Where messages go and what systems are connected
paragraphs: Messages sent through the live forms are processed by the site's server-side enquiry endpoint and written into the site's enquiry storage for manual handling by the team.
Every form also offers a public email fallback. If someone chooses email instead of the form, their message is handled in email rather than through the site form flow.

At launch, the repo does not declare a separate CRM, newsletter platform, partner-management tool, volunteer platform, map embed, or privacy-impacting analytics integration for these collection routes.
bullets: Internal review by the EncouragingYou team
Separate escalation only where a safeguarding concern needs it
No sale of personal information
No automated decision-making or profiling declared in the launch build

sections.title: How long information is kept
paragraphs: The launch build uses a manual review approach rather than an automated deletion schedule. That means messages should not be kept longer than needed for the purpose they were collected for.

General support, involvement, volunteer, and partner messages should be reviewed and deleted once the conversation is complete unless there is a continuing service, safeguarding, screening, referral, or legal reason to retain a limited record.
Safeguarding records may need a different and longer approach because people may need to evidence what was reported, how it was handled, and whether any follow-up was required.

note.title: Why there is no single public number of days here
note.body: The repo does not yet enforce an approved fixed retention schedule. This notice therefore describes the real launch approach: review records regularly and do not keep them just in case.

sections.title: Cookies, analytics, maps, and embeds
paragraphs: The live collection routes do not declare non-essential analytics, advertising tags, or social-feed embeds in the repo at launch.

The site does run a first-party aggregate analytics layer for service-improvement questions such as route progression, successful form completion, and calendar-download interest. That layer does not receive names, email addresses, phone numbers, free-text messages, or safeguarding detail.
The contact route does not load an interactive map on arrival, and the site keeps Rochdale as the public location anchor instead of publishing an exact blanket venue address.
The Cookie Notice explains the current storage and access position in more detail, including why no banner is shown at launch.
If non-essential cookies, profiling analytics, or external processors are added later, this notice and the Cookie Notice must be updated before that change is switched on.
bullets: Non-essential cookies: absent at launch
Analytics platform: first-party and aggregate only
Interactive map on contact route: absent at launch
Social-feed embed on core collection routes: absent at launch
Cookie banner / preference centre: not shown at launch

sections.title: Your choices and rights
paragraphs: You can ask what personal information we still hold about you, ask us to correct it, ask us to delete it when we no longer need it, ask us to restrict how it is used in some situations, or object to optional direct updates.

Some requests may need a careful response rather than an automatic yes. For example, we may need to keep or limit disclosure of information where a safeguarding matter, legal duty, or live case-handling need still applies.
If you are not happy with how your information has been handled, you can raise it with us first and you can also complain to the Information Commissioner's Office.

note.title: Updates opt-in
note.body: The optional updates tick box is separate from the main message. If you do not tick it, your enquiry can still be handled normally.

sections.title: When this notice must change
paragraphs: This notice must be reviewed whenever the site starts collecting a new field, connects a new processor, expands the analytics event scope, adds non-essential cookies or profiling analytics, changes its safeguarding handling, or agrees a fixed retention schedule.
The aim is to keep the privacy notice tied to the real build, not to future plans.

systemsSection.title: What systems are and are not connected at launch.
systemsSection.summary: These states are intentionally explicit so Prompt 36 and later prompts cannot silently introduce a mismatch.

systems.label: Secure form endpoint and enquiry storage
systems.detail: Form submissions are handled by the site's server-side enquiry endpoint and written into enquiry storage configured for the site environment.

systems.label: Public email fallback
systems.detail: Every form also shows the public email address. Messages sent there are handled in email rather than through the site form flow.

systems.label: Newsletter or marketing platform
systems.detail: The updates opt-in is stored with the enquiry record only. It does not add someone to a separate newsletter tool at launch.

systems.label: CRM or case-management platform
systems.detail: The repo does not declare a separate CRM or case-management integration for contact, volunteer, partner, or safeguarding messages at launch.

systems.label: Analytics and tracking
systems.detail: The live build uses a first-party aggregate analytics layer for service-improvement reporting only. It does not send direct identifiers, free-text form content, or safeguarding detail into analytics events.

systems.label: Interactive maps and social embeds on collection routes
systems.detail: The contact route does not load an interactive map on arrival, and the core collection routes do not rely on social-feed embeds.

rightsSection.title: Simple routes to ask questions, correct information, or challenge how it is used.
rightsSection.summary: This notice uses plain language, but these requests still matter and should be handled seriously.

rightsCards.title: Access and explanation
rightsCards.body: Ask us what information we still hold about you and why we are using it.

rightsCards.title: Correction and restriction
rightsCards.body: Tell us if something is wrong or ask us to pause use in situations where that is appropriate.

rightsCards.title: Deletion and objections
rightsCards.body: Ask us to delete information we no longer need, or object to optional updates and similar non-essential uses.

rightsCards.title: Complaint route
rightsCards.body: Raise it with EncouragingYou first if you can, and you can also complain to the ICO.

reviewSection.title: Future work that must update this notice before release.
reviewSection.summary: These triggers keep the privacy notice accurate as the build evolves.

reviewTriggers: Add any non-essential analytics or cookie-setting technology
Change the cookie or storage-access inventory, or introduce consent record storage
Connect a CRM, case-management platform, newsletter tool, or other external processor
Change what the public forms collect or what anti-abuse data is stored
Publish a fixed retention schedule or new contact identity details
Change the safeguarding intake or escalation workflow
Add non-essential cookies, profiling analytics, or a third-party analytics processor
Change the cookie or storage-access inventory, or introduce consent record storage
Connect a CRM, newsletter tool, or any other external processor
Change the public form fields or stored request metadata

page.title: How accessibility is handled on the live EncouragingYou website.
page.summary: This statement covers the live public site, explains what has been checked so far, and shows the clearest route to ask for help or report a barrier.

statusNote.title: Current statement scope
statusNote.body: This is a launch-ready accessibility statement for the live site build. It does not claim a full independent audit or a formal declaration of complete compliance.

summaryCards.title: What the site aims for
summaryCards.body: The live site is built with WCAG 2.2 AA as the working standard for the public launch build.
bullets: Readable contrast and visible focus states
Keyboard-friendly navigation and disclosure patterns
Clear form labels, helper text, and error states
Reduced-motion-safe behaviour where motion is used

summaryCards.title: What has been checked so far
summaryCards.body: Accessibility has been reviewed through the current build, the shared component system, and the automated checks already wired into the repo.
bullets: Keyboard reachability for skip links and FAQ disclosures
Reduced-motion handling on the live shell
Structured form validation, focus recovery, and no-JS confirmation flow
Browser and responsive checks through Astro and Playwright

summaryCards.title: What is still limited
summaryCards.body: The site is honest about the difference between current checks and a full audit.
bullets: No independent accessibility audit is published yet
External platforms such as Instagram, email clients, and calendar apps are outside the main site's direct control
A full assistive-technology compatibility matrix is not published at launch

contents.label: How the launch build has been checked
How to ask for help or report a barrier

measuresSection.title: Accessibility support is built into the current shell, form system, and content patterns.
measuresSection.summary: The site does not rely on a single accessibility widget or one isolated route. The working expectation is that the core public journeys should be understandable, keyboard reachable, and low-friction by default.

items.title: Structure and navigation
items.body: The main shell includes skip links, breadcrumb support, clear heading hierarchy, and a mobile navigation pattern that can be opened, closed, and escaped from the keyboard.

items.title: Forms and feedback routes
items.body: Live forms use labels above fields, helper text, visible validation, focused error recovery, and a no-JavaScript confirmation fallback instead of depending on placeholder-only labelling or client-side scripting alone.

items.title: Motion, media, and low-JS choices
items.body: Reduced-motion preferences are respected, decorative imagery is kept separate from trust-critical claims, and the launch build avoids heavy third-party embeds such as an auto-loaded interactive map on the contact route.

assessmentSection.title: The current statement is based on repo evidence, not a claim of perfection.
assessmentSection.summary: The live statement distinguishes between what has been tested in the current repo and what still needs deeper review or future auditing.

items.title: Manual launch review
items.body: The current build has been reviewed for keyboard reachability, visible focus treatment, reduced-motion behaviour, responsive layouts, and form handling across the main public routes.

items.title: Automated checks in the repo
items.body: The site runs shared unit checks, Astro validation, and Playwright browser checks as part of the current quality gate. Those checks support the statement, but they are not a substitute for an independent audit.

items.title: Review date
items.body: This statement was reviewed against the live launch build on 23 April 2026.

limitationSection.title: Some boundaries are already known, and they are shown here plainly.
limitationSection.summary: Known limitations are listed to reduce frustration. When there is a workaround or a better route, it should be visible alongside the limitation rather than hidden in support replies.

limitations.title: External platforms are outside the main website's direct control
limitations.body: Instagram, email applications opened from mailto links, and calendar applications opened from .ics files are not part of the core site runtime.
limitations.impact: Accessibility on those platforms depends on the provider and the app someone uses.
limitations.workaround: If an external destination blocks you, use the accessibility feedback form or the published email route and explain what you were trying to do.

limitations.title: Calendar downloads depend on the app that opens them
limitations.body: The session routes provide .ics files as a convenience, but the experience after download is controlled by the calendar app or device, not by the website itself.
limitations.impact: Import behaviour, reminders, and display may vary between devices and calendar tools.
limitations.workaround: Use the session page itself as the source of truth for the next date and contact the team if you need the timing confirmed in another format.

limitations.title: A full assistive-technology compatibility matrix is not published yet
limitations.body: The launch build includes manual and automated checks, but it does not yet publish a named matrix for every browser and assistive technology combination.
limitations.impact: Some combinations may work well without being individually documented in public yet.
limitations.workaround: Report the exact combination that caused difficulty so the team can reproduce it and respond more precisely.

externalSurfacesSection.title: The statement separates the main site from the services it links out to.
externalSurfacesSection.summary: This helps keep the statement truthful. The main site can describe and limit its own behaviour, but it should not overclaim control over platforms or applications it does not run.

items.title: Instagram and other social destinations
items.body: Social links leave the site and move into a third-party platform with its own accessibility, privacy, and account settings.

items.title: Email and calendar apps
items.body: Mailto links and calendar files are provided as practical next-step helpers, but the app that opens afterwards is outside the site's direct control.

items.title: Maps and directions
items.body: The launch contact route does not load an interactive map by default. If a public directions link is added later, it will need its own external-service note and review.

feedbackSection.title: Tell us if something is difficult to use or if you need another format.
feedbackSection.summary: The accessibility route should help people reach a practical response, not just acknowledge a principle.
bullets: Tell us which page or action caused the problem
Tell us what you were trying to do
Tell us what device, browser, or assistive technology you were using if you know it
Tell us if you need the information in another format

note.title: Need a general support reply instead?
note.body: If the issue is mainly about joining a session, getting help, or asking a wider question, the Contact route is still available as the general conversation path.

reviewSection.title: This statement should change when the build changes.
reviewSection.summary: The statement is tied to the live public build, not to future plans or ideal conditions.

reviewTriggers: A new public form, embed, processor, or third-party platform is added
A published directions or map link goes live
A formal accessibility audit is completed
A new known limitation is found on the live site
Core route behaviour changes in a way that affects keyboard, motion, forms, or media

page.title: How this site works, what to expect from it, and where the boundaries are.
page.summary: This page explains the practical rules around using the live EncouragingYou website. It is written for the current public build rather than copied from a large corporate platform.

statusNote.title: Launch policy scope
statusNote.body: This policy is intentionally proportionate to the live launch site. It does not invent account, payment, commenting, or upload rules that do not apply here.

summaryCards.title: What the site is for
summaryCards.body: The live site helps people understand support routes, sessions, updates, safeguarding information, and ways to contact or work with EncouragingYou.
bullets: Public information about routes and support
Low-friction next steps into contact or sessions
Legal and trust information grounded in the live build

summaryCards.title: What the site is not
summaryCards.body: The site is not an emergency service, a social network, or a source of formal legal, medical, or financial advice.
bullets: Urgent danger should go to 999, not a general site message
Session and venue information can change and should be checked on the live route
External platforms linked from the site have their own rules and settings

summaryCards.title: Why this page exists
summaryCards.body: The point of this page is to keep boundaries clear without intimidating people who need help, reassurance, or a first step.
bullets: Acceptable use expectations stay simple
Intellectual-property and reuse boundaries are visible
External-link, download, and accuracy limits are explained near the actual feature set

sections.title: What this site covers
sections.summary: This policy applies to the public EncouragingYou website that is live in the current repo and published under the EncouragingYou name.
paragraphs: The live site is operated for EncouragingYou CIC and is designed to help people understand programmes, sessions, events and updates, volunteering and partnership routes, safeguarding information, and the main ways to contact the team.
It should stay proportionate to the live feature set. If the site later adds accounts, payments, public commenting, uploads, or other higher-risk features, this page will need to change.

sections.title: Using the site fairly
sections.summary: Most people will never need to think about this section, but it keeps the basic boundaries visible.
paragraphs: Use the website in a lawful and respectful way. Do not try to break, overload, scrape, impersonate, or misuse the forms, session routes, downloads, or public contact details.
Do not send harmful, misleading, abusive, or deliberately false information through the public enquiry routes.
bullets: Do not use the forms for spam, harassment, or automated bulk submission
Do not pretend to be someone else when asking for support or reporting a concern
Do not try to interfere with the site's security or availability

sections.title: Information, support, and urgency boundaries
sections.summary: The site should help people move toward the right next step, but it cannot guarantee every live detail on every route forever.
paragraphs: Session timing, route detail, and practical information are maintained in good faith from the current structured content model, but public details can change. The live page should be treated as the best current source rather than a guarantee that nothing will move.
The public routes are for information, reassurance, and first contact. They are not a substitute for urgent emergency help, and they should not be treated as legal, financial, or medical advice.
If someone is in immediate danger, use 999 rather than waiting for an ordinary site reply. If the issue is really a safeguarding concern, use the safeguarding route rather than a general enquiry.

note.title: Venue and attendance details
note.body: Some exact venue information is shared on enquiry rather than published as a blanket address on every route. That is part of the live launch approach and may change later if the operational policy changes.

sections.title: External links and third-party destinations
sections.summary: Some next steps leave the main site. When that happens, the outside service controls its own accessibility, privacy, and account behaviour.
paragraphs: The site may link to external destinations such as Instagram, email applications, future map or directions services, or regulatory guidance. Those services are not controlled by the main EncouragingYou website.
A link to an outside service is provided because it is useful, not because EncouragingYou controls or guarantees the service.

cards.title: Social and communication platforms
cards.body: Instagram and email applications have their own accessibility, privacy, moderation, and account rules once you leave the main site.

cards.title: Maps and directions
cards.body: The launch contact route does not auto-load an interactive map. If a public directions link is added later, it will still open an external map service with its own terms and settings.

cards.title: Cookie choices at launch
cards.body: The site does not show a cookie banner at launch because advertising, profiling, and other non-essential tracking are absent. The Cookie Notice is the live source of truth for the narrow first-party analytics model and its objection control.

note.title: Current cookie entry point
note.body: Cookie or storage questions should go through the Cookie Notice route rather than a banner or preferences pop-up, because the current build handles the live analytics objection control on that route.

sections.title: Downloads and calendar files
sections.summary: The current live downloads are limited and practical. They are there to help, not to create a separate content platform.
paragraphs: Where a session route provides an .ics calendar file, that file is intended as a convenience summary of the next session pattern. The live session page should still be treated as the source of truth if details change.

When a calendar file opens in a device calendar application, that application controls the experience afterwards. EncouragingYou does not control the accessibility, reminders, or privacy behaviour of the calendar app itself.
bullets: Check the session page again if you are unsure whether a date or time has changed
Use the contact route if you need the next date confirmed in another format

sections.title: Content, brand, and reuse
sections.summary: The site's copy, branding, and launch media are provided for visiting, sharing links, and understanding the work. They are not published as free stock assets or general-purpose material.
paragraphs: EncouragingYou, the site brand, the site copy, and the design work created for the site should be treated as belonging to the organisation or used with permission where that matters.

Normal public behaviour such as linking to a page, sharing a route, or quoting a short extract in context is different from reproducing the site's full copy, branded assets, or launch illustration system as if they were your own.
bullets: Do not reuse the brand, logo, or launch media in a misleading way
Ask before reusing substantial copy or design assets outside ordinary sharing and referral

sections.title: Policy changes and questions
sections.summary: This page should be updated when the live site grows beyond its current launch footprint.
paragraphs: The policy should be reviewed if the site adds analytics, accounts, public uploads, payments, embedded services, or any other feature that changes the practical boundaries described here.
If you have a question about how the site works or whether a route is the right one to use, contact the team rather than guessing from a partial signal.

note.title: Related routes
note.body: Questions about personal information belong in the Privacy Notice. Questions about accessibility barriers belong in the Accessibility Statement or the dedicated feedback form on that route.

page.title: How this site uses cookies and other storage or access technologies.
page.summary: The live build avoids advertising, profiling, and other non-essential tracking. It does use a tightly limited first-party measurement layer for aggregate site-improvement statistics, together with a simple objection control on this route.

statusNote.title: Launch state
statusNote.body: The live build does not run advertising, remarketing, or third-party analytics. It does run first-party aggregate measurement for service improvement only, and this route is the place to review or switch that measurement off.

summaryCards.title: No advertising or profiling cookies at launch
summaryCards.body: The public routes do not declare advertising pixels, remarketing cookies, third-party analytics tags, or cross-site profiling widgets.

summaryCards.title: No banner at launch
summaryCards.body: The live measurement layer is designed around a narrow statistical-purpose model with an objection control on this route, not a pre-enabled marketing stack that needs a banner.

summaryCards.title: Future additions stay off first
summaryCards.body: Any future analytics vendor, embed, campaign tag, or consent-requiring technology must update this notice, the Privacy Notice, and the implementation before release.

activeSection.title: What the launch build actually does on a visitor's device.
activeSection.summary: These are the audited device-side behaviors in the live public build. None of them activate advertising, profiling, or third-party tracking.

absentSection.title: What is confirmed absent at launch.
absentSection.summary: These common cookie and tracking patterns were checked and are not active in the live build. If any of them are introduced later, this notice and the Privacy Notice must change first.

sections.title: The live launch position is intentionally narrow.
paragraphs: The public build does not use advertising cookies, remarketing tags, social-feed embeds, interactive map embeds, or third-party consent tooling on arrival.

It does use a first-party measurement layer that sends only limited page, CTA, form, and calendar-intent events to the site's own analytics endpoint so the organisation can understand whether key journeys are working and improve the site.
bullets: No advertising, remarketing, or cross-site tracking at launch
No third-party analytics or tag manager at launch
No map or social-feed embed on public routes at launch
No names, email addresses, phone numbers, message text, or safeguarding details in analytics payloads

note.title: External destinations are link-only
note.body: Public links such as Instagram, email, or phone only open if someone chooses to follow them. The site does not embed those services into the page first.

sections.title: Why the launch site does not show a banner.
paragraphs: The live build does not use advertising, profiling, or other non-essential tracking. The only measurement that is active is a narrow first-party aggregate statistics layer intended to understand site journeys and improve the service.

That measurement is paired with clear information and a simple way to object on this route. If a future change introduces analytics or embeds that fall outside that narrow model, the feature must stay switched off until the site has updated notices and the right control mechanism.
bullets: Advertising, profiling, and cross-site tracking stay off
Anonymous service-improvement measurement can be switched off here
Any new purpose or provider requires a fresh review before release

sections.title: How analytics objection works on the live build.
paragraphs: The live analytics layer uses the site's own endpoint and keeps to aggregate service-improvement questions such as which routes lead people to the next step, which forms complete successfully, and which session pages create real interest.

If you switch that measurement off on this route, the site stores only a first-party preference cookie so it can remember your objection on later visits. It does not create an advertising or cross-site identifier.
bullets: No session replay, heatmaps, fingerprinting, or advertising pixels
No free-text form content or personal contact details in analytics events
No safeguarding-route analytics beyond ordinary service delivery

sections.title: Future additions cannot activate silently.
paragraphs: This registry is the change-control surface for device-side technologies. If the implementation changes, this page, the Privacy Notice, and any supporting consent controls must be updated together before the new behavior goes live.

analyticsControlSection.title: Control the live anonymous measurement layer.
analyticsControlSection.summary: These controls only affect the site's own aggregate service-improvement analytics. They do not switch on advertising, remarketing, or third-party tracking.
analyticsControlSection.note: If you ask us to keep the objection in place, the site stores a first-party preference cookie so it can remember that choice on later visits.

active.title: Anonymous service-improvement analytics are active.
active.body: The live build is counting limited route, CTA, form-success, and calendar-interest signals in aggregate so the organisation can improve the site without tracking people across services.

objected.title: Anonymous service-improvement analytics are off on this device.
objected.body: The site is honoring the objection stored for this browser and is not sending live measurement events.

browserSignal.title: Anonymous service-improvement analytics are off because your browser is signaling a privacy preference.
browserSignal.body: The site treats a browser privacy signal as an objection and leaves the live measurement layer switched off.

disabled.title: Anonymous service-improvement analytics are not active in this build.
disabled.body: The runtime is currently configured without live measurement collection.

changeControlSection.title: Before anything non-essential goes live.
changeControlSection.summary: These triggers block silent rollout of new storage, access, or tracking behavior.

changeTriggers: Add analytics, measurement scripts, or campaign tags that store or access information on a device
Add an interactive map, video, social feed, or other embed that brings third-party storage or access behavior into the page
Add a cookie banner, preference centre, or consent record without updating this notice and the Privacy Notice
Start using localStorage, sessionStorage, IndexedDB, or fingerprinting for anything beyond a narrow exempt purpose
Add a CAPTCHA or other third-party anti-abuse token that stores information on the visitor's device

active.label: Standard browser request delivery
active.purpose: Load HTML, CSS, JavaScript, fonts, images, and other same-site files in the normal way a website works.
active.implementation: The site relies on ordinary browser requests and same-site responses. It does not add a tracking identifier or consent cookie to those responses.
active.duration: No site-defined storage is written to the device by this mechanism.

active.label: Same-site enquiry submission handling
active.purpose: Send a message to the site's own `/api/enquiry/` endpoint when someone chooses to submit a form.
active.implementation: The form posts `FormData` to the same site. Anti-abuse checks use a honeypot field, a render-time check, and request metadata on the server instead of a client-side cookie or third-party token.
active.duration: Nothing is stored on the device by this mechanism.
active.userControl: It only runs if a visitor submits a form.

active.label: Responsive layout and reduced-motion adaptation
active.purpose: Adapt layout, navigation state, and motion level to the screen size and reduced-motion preference already exposed by the browser or device.
active.implementation: CSS media queries and `window.matchMedia(...)` help the shell respond to viewport width and `prefers-reduced-motion` without storing a cross-visit preference record.
active.duration: The result is not stored beyond the current page view.
active.userControl: Visitors can change the underlying browser or device preference in their own settings.

active.label: First-party aggregate service-improvement analytics
active.purpose: Count limited route, CTA, calendar-intent, and form-completion signals in aggregate so the organisation can understand whether the public site is helping people take the next step.
active.implementation: The shared client runtime and server routes send only a small allowlisted event taxonomy to the site's own `/api/analytics/` endpoint. The endpoint stores aggregate counters only and rejects free text, direct identifiers, safeguarding details, and third-party sharing.
active.duration: No measurement identifier is written to the device. Aggregate daily counters are retained server-side for service-improvement reporting.
active.userControl: The objection control on this route turns the measurement layer off for this browser.

active.label: Analytics objection preference cookie
active.purpose: Remember an objection to the live aggregate analytics layer so the site does not restart that measurement on later visits from the same browser.
active.implementation: A first-party cookie is only set when someone actively turns the anonymous analytics layer off. It stores the objection state and is not reused for advertising, personalization, or cross-site tracking.
active.duration: Up to 180 days unless it is cleared sooner or the visitor turns the measurement back on.
active.userControl: Visitors can clear the objection by using the control on this route.

absent.label: Non-essential cookies
absent.detail: The live build does not declare first-party or third-party cookies for advertising, remarketing, personalization, or cross-site analytics.
absent.reason: The launch measurement model is intentionally limited to first-party aggregate statistics and an objection cookie.

absent.label: Web storage APIs
absent.detail: The public site code does not use `localStorage`, `sessionStorage`, or IndexedDB to remember analytics choices or identify people between visits.
absent.reason: The live preference control uses a first-party cookie instead of browser storage APIs.

absent.label: Analytics and measurement tags
absent.detail: No third-party analytics platform, tag manager, heatmap, session replay tool, or measurement pixel is configured on the public routes.
absent.reason: The live measurement model is intentionally same-site and processor-free at launch.

absent.label: Interactive maps and third-party embeds
absent.detail: The contact route does not load an interactive map on arrival, and the public routes do not embed social feeds, videos, or third-party widgets that set storage on page load.
absent.reason: Third-party embed behavior is kept out of the page until there is a verified need and a lawful implementation path.

absent.label: Advertising, remarketing, and cross-site tracking
absent.detail: No advertising pixel, remarketing tag, affiliate cookie, or cross-site profiling technology is active in the launch build.
absent.reason: The site does not run advertising or remarketing at launch.

absent.label: Third-party anti-abuse tokens
absent.detail: The form stack does not use reCAPTCHA, Turnstile, or another third-party token that stores or accesses information on the visitor's device.
absent.reason: Launch abuse defenses are handled through same-site validation and server-side checks instead.

futureGuardrails.title: Statistical or analytics additions
futureGuardrails.body: Any future analytics tool must be classified explicitly, checked against the relevant PECR exception, reflected in the registry, and kept off until the site can support the right notice and user control.

futureGuardrails.title: Embeds, maps, and third-party widgets
futureGuardrails.body: Any future embed must be configured in the most privacy-preserving mode available, or replaced with a plain link if the provider cannot support a truthful launch-safe implementation.

futureGuardrails.title: Banner and preference rollout
futureGuardrails.body: If a future feature requires consent, the site must move from this objection-based route to a real banner and preference flow with equal reject and accept emphasis, durable withdrawal, and a persistent revisit path.

immediateDanger.title: Immediate danger needs emergency services first
immediateDanger.message: If someone is in immediate danger, has been seriously harmed, or needs urgent emergency help, call 999 first.
immediateDanger.summary: A web form or email should not be treated as an emergency-response service.

publicConcernRoute.summary: For launch, safeguarding concerns can be sent through the secure concern form or directly to the public safeguarding inbox. Use only the detail needed to explain the concern and how to contact you back.
publicConcernRoute.responseBoundary: This route is monitored for safeguarding concerns, but the site does not promise an immediate reply, live case updates, or emergency intervention through the form itself.
publicConcernRoute.handoffNote: If you are unsure whether the concern belongs under child or adult safeguarding, use the route that feels closest and explain what you know. The team can route it internally.

secureConcernForm.summary: A dedicated secure concern form is available for non-emergency safeguarding messages and routes into the same public safeguarding intake as the email inbox.
secureConcernForm.boundaryNote: Do not wait for an online reply if the risk is immediate or emergency help is needed.

policyDocument.title: Full safeguarding policy
policyDocument.summaryWhenAvailable: When a public safeguarding policy PDF is approved for launch, it can be linked here without changing the route family or contact pattern.
policyDocument.summaryWhenUnavailable: A fuller public safeguarding policy is not linked yet. The live site should stay honest about that instead of pretending a download is already available.

vettingAndTraining.summary: The approved public brief states that safeguarding procedures are in place and followed, and that staff and volunteers are trained and vetted.
bullets: Safeguarding procedures are in place and followed.
Staff and volunteers are trained and vetted.
Public trust should come from clear boundaries, not from overclaiming what has not been published yet.

preparationChecklist: Say whether the concern is about a child, young person, or adult.
Share what you saw, heard, or were told, and when it happened or became known.
Include only the information needed to understand the concern and contact you back.
If the risk is urgent, call 999 instead of waiting for a response from the site.

generalContactBoundary.title: General contact is not the safeguarding route
generalContactBoundary.body: Use the normal Contact route for ordinary questions, volunteering, partnership discussions, or general support requests. Use safeguarding when the issue is about harm, abuse, neglect, exploitation, coercion, or someone not being safe.

proofBoundary.title: Public safeguarding proof boundary
proofBoundary.summary: The public site should explain the safeguarding basics clearly while holding back anything that still needs operational confirmation or formal approval.
publishNow: Separate child and adult safeguarding routes are visible in the public navigation.
Immediate danger is directed to emergency services rather than a website form.
The approved brief supports the statement that safeguarding procedures are in place and that staff and volunteers are trained and vetted.
awaitingConfirmation: A publicly named safeguarding lead or role contact.
A published response-time expectation for safeguarding messages.
A public downloadable safeguarding policy document.
withheldUntilVerified: Any personal phone number or named contact that has not been approved for launch.
Claims about confidentiality, investigation steps, or case outcomes that have not been formally defined.
Local escalation contacts or statutory referral wording that the supplied launch materials do not verify.

routes.title: Child safeguarding
routes.summary: Use this route when the concern is about a child or young person who may be at risk of abuse, neglect, exploitation, grooming, unsafe treatment, or serious welfare harm.
appliesTo: A child or young person saying they are not safe.
A worrying disclosure, injury, pattern of neglect, or unsafe adult behaviour.
Concerns about exploitation, grooming, coercion, or online harm.
signals: Act quickly when the concern is serious.

routes.audienceLabel: Adult with care and support needs
routes.title: Adult safeguarding
routes.summary: Use this route when the concern is about an adult with care and support needs who may be experiencing abuse, neglect, coercion, exploitation, or another serious risk to their safety or wellbeing.
appliesTo: A serious concern about abuse, neglect, coercion, or unsafe treatment.
Worry that someone is being exploited, controlled, or is unable to protect themselves.
A situation where an adult's care and support needs make the risk more serious.
signals: Use the safeguarding route rather than ordinary contact.

child.title: Child safeguarding
child.summary: This route is for concerns about a child or young person. It keeps the emergency boundary, the reporting route, and the basic safeguarding expectations visible without turning the page into a dense policy document.
child.appliesToTitle: Use the child safeguarding route when
appliesTo: the concern is about a child or young person under 18
you are worried about abuse, neglect, exploitation, grooming, or unsafe treatment
a disclosure, incident, or pattern of behaviour makes you think a child may not be safe
child.escalationSummary: The aim is to make the next step obvious without implying the website replaces emergency or statutory action.

steps.title: Call 999 first if the risk is immediate
steps.body: If the child or young person is in immediate danger, needs urgent medical help, or cannot be kept safe right now, call 999 before using any site route.

steps.title: Raise the concern as soon as possible
steps.body: Use the secure concern form or the public safeguarding inbox instead of waiting for a fuller story or a more polished account.

steps.title: Keep the information factual
steps.body: Say what you saw, heard, or were told, when it happened or became known, and who was involved if you know.

steps.title: Do not promise secrecy you cannot keep
steps.body: If someone has shared a concern with you, explain that you may need to pass it to the right safeguarding people to help keep them safe.

child.preparationTitle: Before you send a child safeguarding concern
preparationItems: Use names, ages, or relationship details only if they matter to understanding the concern.
Say where the concern happened or who else may already know.
Be clear about any immediate risk, recent incident, or urgent welfare need.
Send the concern even if you do not know every detail.
child.limitationsTitle: What this page does not promise
limitations: It does not promise an instant reply or live emergency support.
It does not replace emergency services or statutory safeguarding action.
It does not publish a full policy download or named lead until those details are approved.
child.concernNoteTitle: Still use the safeguarding route if you are unsure
child.concernNoteBody: If you are not completely certain whether the concern belongs under child or adult safeguarding, use the route that feels closest and explain why. Clear reporting matters more than choosing the perfect label.

adult.title: Adult safeguarding
adult.summary: This route is for concerns about an adult with care and support needs. It keeps the route practical and serious while separating adult safeguarding clearly from child safeguarding and ordinary contact.
adult.appliesToTitle: Use the adult safeguarding route when
appliesTo: the concern is about an adult with care and support needs
you are worried about abuse, neglect, coercion, exploitation, or unsafe circumstances
the adult's situation means they may be unable to protect themselves or get help safely
adult.escalationSummary: The public route should make the adult safeguarding boundary clear without implying the site is a crisis service.

steps.title: Call 999 first if there is immediate danger
steps.body: If the adult is in immediate danger, seriously injured, or needs urgent emergency help, call 999 before using the site.

steps.title: Use the safeguarding route, not ordinary contact
steps.body: Send the concern through the secure safeguarding form or the public safeguarding inbox so it reaches the right intake route.

steps.title: Share the concern plainly and proportionately
steps.body: Explain what is happening, why you are worried, and what immediate risk or support need you know about without adding unnecessary personal detail.

steps.title: Say if you think the adult may struggle to stay safe
steps.body: If care and support needs, coercion, isolation, or dependency make the risk more serious, include that context so the concern is understood properly.

adult.preparationTitle: Before you send an adult safeguarding concern
preparationItems: Describe the risk, the immediate situation, and any urgent welfare concern.
Explain how you know about the concern and whether anyone else is already involved.
Include contact details for yourself so the team can respond if needed.
Use the child safeguarding route instead if the concern is really about someone under 18.
adult.limitationsTitle: What this page does not promise
limitations: It does not replace emergency or statutory adult-safeguarding action.
It does not promise confidential handling terms or response times that have not been publicly defined.
It does not ask people to decide complicated case thresholds before raising a serious concern.
adult.concernNoteTitle: Report the concern even if you do not have every answer
adult.concernNoteBody: Clear concern reporting matters more than having a complete picture. If the situation is serious, raise it with the facts you have rather than waiting for certainty.

First-time reassurance stays visible before someone attends
Contact remains available if you want to ask first

supportPoints: Referrals, sponsorship, and collaboration all start here
A formal proposal is not required for first contact
The next step stays proportionate and enquiry-led

content.nextStep: Use the contact route, choose a referral-focused reason, and tell us whether you are asking on someone else's behalf.

supportPoints: You do not need to know the exact programme before asking
Parents, carers, schools, and partners can all start here

content.title: Support in another practical way
content.summary: If you can offer space, introductions, practical help, or local support that does not fit a named role yet, start here.
content.nextStep: Use a live public opportunity when one is published, or send a short message describing the support you have in mind.
content.trustNote: The hub should stay open to practical help without turning every supporter into a fundraiser or sending them through the wrong route.
supportPoints: Current public opportunities should surface when they are live
Contact remains the fallback if your offer does not fit a named path
